* Managing projects to deliver high quality services and deliverables in accordance with the business procedures.
* Preparing and presenting order of cost estimates and option studies.
* Cost planning.
* Cost-in-use studies - study of the cost of building maintenance.
* Advising on and implementing procurement strategies.
* Preparing tender documentation and managing the tender process, including designing tender marking schemes.
* Preparing bids for services.
* Evaluating and reporting on tenders.
* Valuing completed work and arranging for payments.
* Settling final accounts.
* Providing technical advice on legal and contractual issues relating to construction projects.
* Administrating contracts as Contract Administrator or Employer’s Agent.
* Producing and presenting reports to Customers.
* Mentoring and coaching employees to their full potential.
* Managing service delivery for profit.
* Advising Directors promptly of any issues that have arisen, or that might arise, on projects; in particular those that might impact on Gleeds professional indemnity (PI) insurance



**As a Gleeds team member, you will have access to:** 



* Opportunities to develop and grow your career
* Employee Assistance Programme



  





**Who we’re looking for:** 




**Experience, Knowledge And Key Skills** 



* Sound cost management experience in major construction projects covering pre-contract and construction / post-contract cost management tasks - real estate, manufacturing industry
* Practical knowledge of construction procurement strategies, including tendering and contract strategies.
* Understanding of legislation impacting on building contracts.
* Clear and effective communication skills - both oral and written
* Methodical way of thinking and approach to work
* Organisational skills and the ability to quickly adapt to changing environments.
* Excellent problem, negotiating, finance and numeracy management skills
* Sound ICT skills, with a good level of proficiency in MS Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint;
* Ability to work as part of a team.
* Fluent English. Swedish is an asset.
* Eligible to work in Sweden with a solid knowledge of the Nordic markets.



**Qualifications** 



* QS, Engineering, Architecture degree or technical qualification in construction-related field



  





**A world of opportunity** 




 Gleeds is a place to be part of something bigger. We take pride in our diverse and supportive culture that empowers you to make a difference while creating connections with brilliant people – colleagues and clients alike. Enjoy doing work that shapes our collective future, while steering your own journey in a trusting environment. Benefit from our rich heritage and global opportunities as you construct a career with purpose.
 



 With over 75 offices across the globe, you’ll become part of a truly global team that isn’t restricted by borders. We welcome new perspectives and ideas but above all, we believe that quality relationships sit at the heart of good business. We strive to be the people that seek to understand and bring people together. Wherever we are in the world, we’re committed to the success of our people, clients and communities – giving everyone a safer and more sustainable place to live, work and thrive.
 



 Our values underpin what we stand for and how we work:
 


* Professionalism with personality
* Excellence with humility
* Innovation with agility



**We’re an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications regardless of age, disability, marital status, race, religion or belief, sexual orientation, transgender status or gender.** 




**We recognise the importance of work/life balance and agree flexible working arrangements to suit each individual.** 




**Gleeds is a Great Place to Work certified employer.**

title: Acne Studios Warehouse Manager SE
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone's contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimise our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As Warehouse Manager SE, you play a key role in translating these ambitions into operational excellence. You lead and develop the warehouse operation to deliver high‑quality logistics performance across all channels, while creating a safe, inclusive and motivating environment where people can grow and perform at their best. You will be based at our Warehouse in Spånga.
   

  

 With a forward‑looking and hands‑on mindset, you take full ownership of both strategic development and daily execution. You drive continuous improvement across processes, structure and ways of working, ensuring a flexible, efficient and scalable warehouse operation that supports Acne Studios' continued growth.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As Warehouse Manager SE, you translate strategic direction into strong operational performance while building a flexible and scalable logistics setup that supports Acne Studios' growth. You lead the long‑term development of the warehouse while staying closely connected to daily operations, ensuring high standards, efficiency and strong team performance by:
   

  

* Owning the end‑to‑end management of Warehouse SE, including all core warehouse flows (inbound, outbound, returns, e‑tail and special flows) and related operational and administrative processes.
* Leading, developing and engaging the warehouse team through recruitment, planning, training and hands‑on daily leadership, while securing accurate time reporting in Quinyx.
* Remaining operationally involved by regularly participating in warehouse activities to ensure deep process understanding and continuous improvement.
* Driving performance and collaboration through regular performance follow‑ups, annual reviews and close partnerships with internal and external stakeholders.
* Managing supplier and procurement activities, including contract evaluation, invoice approval in MediusFlow and cost control in line with budget expectations.
* Working closely with wholesale, retail and e‑tail teams to deliver a seamless, high‑quality logistics experience across all channels.
* Ensuring accurate stock handling through returns management, discrepancy investigations, regular stock counts and the annual full inventory.
* Securing compliance with Health & Safety regulations and following up on warehouse KPIs together with the Supply Chain Manager EMEA.


**Who You Are**
 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

**Specific Personal Qualities For This Role**
* Leads by example with a hands‑on, execution‑focused mindset
* Communicates clearly and builds trust across teams
* Takes strong ownership and follows through
* Solves problems with structure and pragmatism
* Fosters an inclusive, high‑performing team culture
* Self‑driven, business‑minded and collaborative


**Specific Experience And Knowledge For This Role**
* Bachelor's degree in business, engineering or a related field
* At least five years of leadership experience in a labor‑intensive environment
* Minimum three years of hands‑on experience in warehouse operations
* Proven ability to optimize processes and manage daily operations
* Fluent communication skills in Swedish and English
* Strong analytical, numerical and systems proficiency (ERP, WMS, MS Office)


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.
   

  

 A background check will be conducted on the final candidate as part of our standard recruitment process.

title: Technical Sales Engineer - Power
description:
**Job Description**
 In the
 **Technical UPS Sales Engineer** 
 role, you will provide technical support for sales across various Lines of Business in Vertiv, working as a part of a large team that may interface with local and regional customers. In this role you will provide Sales with technical support in building proposals, quotes and tenders, with direct responsibility on local accounts or projects, but also enable the development of technically and price competitive applications for products to fulfil customer requirements where standard products do not fit.
   

  

 You will need to have the technical competence to understand and demonstrate the Vertiv offering and our capabilities for your specific segment. Your targets will be set to align with the growth expectations of the market and Vertiv strategic position with the respective accounts.
   

  

 Technical Sales have a key role in Vertiv and consequently we are looking for a highly competent, energetic, and ambitious individual to take on this role within the organization, learn and develop skills across different products, lines of business, customers, and regions. You will be part of a large team of Application Engineers and Technical Sales Experts with clear competencies, career paths and opportunities to take your career to the next level.
   

  

**As a Technical Sales Engineer, You Shall**
* Provide complex presales technical support by reviewing the RFx and identifying customer requirements/specifications.
* Coordinate with factory engineering departments for specific technical requests.
* Design and deliver innovative solutions, rethinking current standards and routines to achieve competitive advantages in the market. Successfully initiate and sustain the necessary changes, managing internal stakeholders and securing support.
* Evaluate special requests that don’t fit standard solutions and propose complex customizable options (Configure to Order)
* Manage the relationship between the various stakeholder groups in Vertiv (Sales teams, Engineering Teams and others)
* Create and perform product and application presentations for customers and consultants.
* Provide internal trainings for colleagues in Vertiv and external trainings for customers
* Manage In-country activities (ICA), related to Application Engineering tasks
* Anticipate market trends being an Industry expert.
* Ensure that any activity, task, and responsibility required is managed and performed in full compliance with the Company Code of Conduct and Trade Compliance.
* Collect and act based on voice of the customers


**You Have**
* Solid technical UPS background and Industry expertise and Electrical education
* Engineering curiosity. Passion to learn every single day and stay ahead of the curve. You are results oriented.
* Knowledge of the assigned segment and industry in EMEA.
* Excellent commercial experience to understand and anticipate market trends, and Vertiv’s business environment.
* Minimum of 5 years of proven records in Offering, Engineering, Pre-Sales, Product Marketing
* Communications and interpersonal skills
* You are fluent in English and your local language, and ideally speak a 2 nd European Language
* Excellent IT skills (Word/Excel/PowerPoint) and Customer/Sales orientation. CAD proficiency is an advantage.
* Previous working experience: experience in products and installation, especially for Datacentre or equivalent


**About Us**
*The successful candidate will embrace Vertiv’s Core Principals & Behaviors to help execute our Strategic Priorities.*
**OUR CORE PRINCIPALS:** 
 Safety. Integrity. Respect. Teamwork. Diversity & Inclusion.
   

  

**OUR STRATEGIC PRIORITIES**
* Customer Focus
* Operational Excellence
* High-Performance Culture
* Innovation
* Financial Strength


***OUR BEHAVIORS***
* Own It
* Act With Urgency
* Foster a Customer-First Mindset
* Think Big and Execute
* Lead by Example
* Drive Continuous Improvement
* Learn and Seek Out Development


*At Vertiv, we offer the stability of a global leader in a growing industry and the opportunity of a startup. We design, manufacture and service the mission-critical infrastructure technologies for vital applications in data centers, communication networks and commercial and industrial environments. With $5 billion in sales, a strong customer base and global reach in nearly 70 countries, our move to establish a standalone business enables us to deliver greater value to our customers and create new opportunities for our people.*
*Vertiv is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression (including transgender status or sexual stereotypes), genetic information, citizenship status, national origin, protected veteran status, political affiliation, or disability. If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, you can request help by sending an email to help.join@vertivco.com . If you are interested in applying or learning more about this role, please visit the company’s career page located on Vertiv.com/Careers*
**About The Team**
 Equal Opportunity Employer
   

  

 We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression (including transgender status or sexual stereotypes), genetic information, citizenship status, national origin, protected veteran status, political affiliation, or disability.

title: Business Analyst
description:
**Företagsbeskrivning**
 AFRY erbjuder tjänster inom teknik, design, digitalisering och rådgivning. Vi är dedikerade experter inom industri, energi och infrastruktur och skapar värde för kommande generationer. AFRY har nordiska rötter med global räckvidd. Våra gemensamma principer – Result driven, Client centric, Empowering och Accountable – vägleder oss i hur vi arbetar, utvecklas och lyckas tillsammans. Var med och accelerera övergången till ett hållbart samhälle.
   

  

**Jobbeskrivning**
 Vi söker en erfaren Business Analyst för uppdrag inom ett flertal branscher och med stort fokus på offentlig sektor, energi- och försvarsindustrin. Arbetet bygger på ett stort intresse för människor, teknik och bryggan där emellan. Konsultrollen innebär i mångt och mycket att vara en rådgivande part gentemot våra kunder och bygga förtroende.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att arbeta nära kunder för att samla in och analysera affärskrav, hantera intressenter och driva processförbättringar. Du ansvarar för att identifiera risker, analysera data för bättre beslutsfattande och säkerställa smidig implementering av IT-lösningar. Dessutom stödjer du förändringshantering och ser till att nya system och processer integreras effektivt i verksamheten. Genom din förmåga att kommunicera och skapa förtroende samarbetar du med intressenter på alla nivåer, från operativ till strategisk nivå.
   

  

**Kvalifikationer**
* Examen inom IT, datavetenskap eller affärsadministration (master är meriterande).
* 3–5 års erfarenhet av affärsanalys och IT-projekt.
* Kunskap i Agile, Scrum och analysverktyg som SQL, Excel, Power BI.
* Starkt analytiskt tänkande, problemlösningsförmåga och god kommunikation.
* Erfarenhet av projektledning och affärsprocesser.
* Flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift.


 Det är meriterande om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av agil utveckling.
* Certifieringar som CBAP/ECBA är meriterande.
* Konsulterfarenhet.
* Kravarbete i förstudiefas.
* Kunskap om LOU. Erfarenhet inom energi och/eller försvarsmakt


 Vi lägger stor vikt på dina personliga egenskaper. Du är en relationsbyggare som säkerställer bra samarbete och har förståelse för kunds behov. Du hanterar stakeholder management väl och etablerar förtroende. Vidare är du affärsinriktad och nätverkande, ser potentiellt mervärde för kund och lyfter förslag genom tydlig kommunikation och presentation.
   

  

**Ytterligare information**
 Som anställd hos oss kommer du att ingå i ett team med härliga kollegor där våra kärnvärden genomsyrar vårt dagliga arbete. För oss innebär det en gemenskap och ett driv att utmana oss själva och våra kunder i att utvecklas och bli starkare. Vårt team består av hjälpsamma och omtänksamma kollegor där vi värderar gemenskap och balans mellan arbete och fritid.
   

  

 Gruppen består idag av 25 konsulter med erfarenhet som sträcker sig från några år till uppemot 25 år, i olika roller och inom flera sektorer. Vår gemensamma drivkraft är viljan att driva och koordinera för att stötta våra kunder utifrån olika utmaningar samt möjligheter.
   

  

**Varför jobba med mig? – Fredrik, rekryterande chef:** 
 Som ledare är jag närvarande, stöttande och möjliggör framgång genom att ta bort hinder. Jag drivs av att prestera tillsammans och imponeras dagligen av vilken potential som finns hos varje medarbetare i gruppen. Mitt främsta mål är att vi som grupp ska leverera till våra kunder, samtidigt som vi har grymt kul på jobbet, vilket bygger på att alla tar ansvar för både leverans och teamwork.
   

  

 Hos oss får du möjligheten att ta del av:
   

  

* Möjlighet till hybridarbete om uppdrag/kund inte kräver annat
* Individuell kompetensutvecklingsplan inom ditt spetsområde
* En AFRY Buddy som bollplank under din första tid som anställd
* Tillgång till stort utbud aktiviteter genom vår sociala klubb: Club AFRY, till exempel yoga, restaurangbesök, språkkurser med mera
* Konkurrenskraftiga förmåner gällande pension, friskvård och föräldraledighet reglerade i vårt kollektivavtal
* Företagsrabatter med externa partners genom vår förmånsportal Advantage You
* Ett enormt nätverk av tekniska experter med + 18 000 kollegor med kontor i 50 och projekt i 100 länder


**Välkommen med din ansökan, sista ansökningsdag är 2026-04-20**
**Kontaktperson för frågor:**
 Fredrik Lindblad, Business Section Manager
   

  

 fredrik.f.lindblad@afry.com
   

  

 För samtliga rekryteringar på AFRY genomförs som grund alltid en ID-kontroll, referenstagning samt verifiering av utbildning.
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.
   

  

*På AFRY driver vi förändring i allt vi gör. Vi anser att förändring sker när modiga idéer möts, när vi samarbetar, skapar innovation och omfamnar kreativa lösningar, det är så vi skapar framtiden. Vi söker konstant kvalificerade kandidater som vill ansluta sig till våra inkluderande team runt om i världen. Bli en del av oss och påskynda den gröna omställningen*

title: Executive Exchange Project Manager
description:
Mentimeter is an engagement tool with a clear goal in mind. To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.
   

  

 We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.
   

  

 Are you someone who makes complex things look effortless? Who cares just as much about the strategy behind an executive dinner as you do about the place cards on the table? Who sees every interaction with a senior leader as a chance to learn something that makes us smarter as a company?
   

  

 If you light up at the idea of orchestrating premium experiences for senior leaders at the world's most influential companies, and you're just as excited about what happens after those experiences — the insights captured, the relationships deepened, the value created — do keep reading.
   

  

**About Mentimeter**
 Over 300 million people have used Mentimeter to participate, engage, and make better decisions together. Now we're building something deeper.
   

  

 Our ambition is to become the operating system for how leaders lead, educators teach, and facilitators run their sessions. To get there, we need to understand the people who shape how the world's largest organizations communicate, learn, and make decisions. Not from a distance, but up close.
   

  

 Executive Exchange is how we do that. It's a strategic function that brings together premium education, intimate executive communities, and deep relationship building with senior leaders at our most important customers. The goal is mutual: we help them become better leaders, and what we learn from them helps us build a better product and service offering for everyone.
   

  

 This role is at the center of it all.
   

  

 The role
   

  

 As Executive Exchange Project Manager, you'll be the operational owner of the entire Executive Exchange function. That spans two areas:
   

  

 The Executive Academy, where we run certification courses and masterclasses grounded in cognitive neuroscience and designed for genuine behavior change. And the Executive Community, which includes Curiosity Club dinners, Advisory Boards, and The Shift, our flagship multi-day conference for senior leaders.
   

  

 You'll turn strategic direction into flawless execution: owning the master calendar, coordinating stakeholders across the business, and ensuring every touchpoint reflects the premium standard our most important customers expect.
   

  

 But this role goes beyond logistics. Every dinner, every course, every interaction with a senior executive is an opportunity to learn: about their goals, their challenges, how they use Mentimeter, and what they need from us next. A core part of your job is making sure those insights don't stay in a notebook. You'll build the systems and habits that turn what we learn into actionable intelligence for our Customer Success, Product, and Marketing teams, so they can strengthen relationships, improve our offering, and create more value for these customers.
   

  

 This is not a supporting role. You own workstreams end-to-end, operate with high autonomy, and are directly accountable for the operational quality of everything we deliver to senior executives at our most important customers.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

* Own the master calendar, production timeline, and logistics for all Executive Exchange programs and events
* Project manage Curiosity Club dinners, Advisory Boards, and The Shift from planning to follow-up
* Coordinate Executive Academy certification courses and masterclasses: enrollment, participant communications, scheduling, venue and tech setup, materials, and certificates
* Build and maintain strong, energizing relationships with Customer Success making and our own senior leader, making it easy for them to engage their accounts in our programs, respectively
* Capture what we learn about participants, their organizations, and their needs, and turn it into structured intelligence that helps Customer Success build stronger relationships, helps Product calibrate our roadmap, and helps leadership make better decisions about where to invest
* Manage engagement data, CRM accuracy, and reporting dashboards
* Own the quality of every participant touchpoint: invitations, landing pages, emails, follow-ups, and event experiences
* Continuously improve operational playbooks as the function scales from pilot to platform


 Who we believe you are
   

  

 You're a senior operational leader who combines sharp project management with genuine warmth and premium taste. You see the strategic picture and execute the details with equal confidence.
   

  

 You don't just manage logistics. You care about the experience, and you care just as much about what happens after. You're the kind of person who thinks about how a dinner conversation becomes an insight that strengthens an account relationship, or how a certification course participant's feedback can sharpen our product strategy. You understand that premium experiences are a means to something bigger: lasting, trust-based relationships that make our customers more successful and our business stronger.
   

  

 You’ll thrive here if you…
   

  

* Have proven experience managing premium events, executive programs, or customer marketing in a B2B enterprise environment
* Have a track record of owning complex, multi-stakeholder initiatives end-to-end
* Are a natural relationship builder, especially with internal teams like Customer Success and Marketing
* Have strong taste and quality instinct: you know what premium looks like and won't settle for less
* Are highly organized and detail-obsessed, while keeping the big picture in view
* Communicate and write confidently in English at a level suited for executive audiences, whether it's an invitation, a post-event summary, or an account intelligence brief
* Are comfortable building systems and processes from scratch in a fast-moving environment
* Are curious about how AI tools can help you work smarter and deliver more personalized experiences at scale
* Get energized by the idea of building an ecosystem that creates real value for customers and drives commercial growth, not just running great events


 Bonus points for…
   

  

* Background in community building or executive exchange programs
* Experience with modern CRM systems and event platforms
* Ability to analyze engagement data and produce clear, actionable reports


 Your impact
   

  

**Within Your First Year, You’ll Have**
* Delivered a full calendar of Executive Academy certification courses, masterclasses, executive dinners, and community events, all at a world-class standard
* Built the operational backbone that lets Executive Exchange scale: playbooks, systems, feedback loops, and stakeholder rhythms
* Become the trusted operational partner for Customer Success and Marketing across Executive Exchange
* Played a direct role in growing Mentimeter's impact with the executives who shape how the world's largest organizations communicate, lead, and learn


 Why Mentimeter
   

  

* Join a strategic initiative with direct C-level visibility and impact
* Work on a beloved product used by over 300 million people
* Profitable, fast-growing international scaleup with a strong culture
* Competitive compensation and benefits, including pension contributions
* A healthy perspective on life balance
* Access to leadership programs and continuous professional development


 This is a dynamic role in a fast-growing area of our business. As Executive Exchange scales, the role will evolve, and you'll play a key role in defining its future.
   

  

 If you don't tick every single box, apply anyway. We want someone who's ready to build something meaningful with us.
   

  

**What Mentimeter Can Offer**
 At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.
   

  

 All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page
   

  

**AI and Hiring at Mentimeter**
 At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.
   

  

* AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
* There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
* Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.


 AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.
   

  

**Culture at Mentimeter**
 At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.
   

  

 Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.
   

  

 Review our Privacy Policy for more information.

title: Senior Manager - Finance Systems
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
 We are looking for a Senior Manager, Finance Systems to lead and scale Legora’s finance technology ecosystem. You will own the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of our finance systems, driving automation, improving financial visibility, and enabling scalable finance operations across multiple regions and entities.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

* Own the strategy and roadmap for Legora’s finance systems and tooling
* Implement, manage, and optimize core finance systems such as ERP, billing, procurement, and reporting platforms
* Manage, oversee and administer any maintenance and upgrades to our ERP system and other finance owned software applications
* Design and manage system integrations and data flows across the finance and business technology stack
* Lead automation and process improvement initiatives across financial close, reporting, and reconciliations
* Partner with Accounting, Finance, Data, and Engineering to ensure systems support key financial processes
* Support scalable financial structures as Legora expands into new markets and entities
* Ensure strong financial data governance, system reliability, and internal controls


 Who You Are
   

  

* 10+ years of experience in finance systems, ERP implementations, or business systems roles, partly in high-growth technology company or startup environments
* Detailed understanding of finance processes including Record-to-Report, Procure-to-Pay, and Order-to-Cash
* Hands-on experience with modern ERP systems with exposure to automation, AI tools, or analytics platforms within finance workflows
* Experience working with SaaS finance stacks, including billing, payments, and reporting tools
* Proven ability to lead cross-functional systems initiatives and complex implementations in an international environment
* Well versed in project management, you are analytical and a trusted collaborator with your stakeholders
* We also value a background in accounting or CPA qualification (or equivalent)


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Influencer Marketing Manager
description:
**Company Description**
*Publicis Groupe is one of the largest communications groups in the world. We are building the agency model of the future and take a holistic approach to our clients’ challenges. By fusing together our media, creative, data and technology expertise, we are able to provide clients with integrated solutions, transformative ideas and compelling consumer experiences. We call it the Power of One.
   

  

 We are proud winner of many awards and nominations, both in Sweden and internationally. But nothing makes us prouder than to work with clients and brands such as P&G, Disney, H&M, Mondelez, Volvo, VISA, Lantmännen, LEGO, Spotify, Perrigo, Renault and many more.
   

  

 At Publicis, we put our people first. We are proud to be certified by Great Place to Work (2022, 2023, 2024, 2025) and have won the Diversity Charter Sweden Awards in the category Mental Health (2025). We have also been recognized with a Magnet Award for our engaging culture (2023). Our goal is to become the leading workplace in our industry. We are based in a fantastic office in Odenplan and offer a flexible work policy.*
**Overview**
 Are you passionate about social media, influencers, and creative campaigns? Do you enjoy client interaction and driving projects with both precision and creativity? Then you might be our next
 **Influencer Marketing Manager** 
 !
   

  

 In this role, you will be the face of our Influencer Marketing department and a key player in developing our clients’ businesses. You will be responsible for receiving and challenging client briefs, ensuring that the team delivers according to high quality standards, and serving as the primary point of contact for selected clients within Influencer Marketing. You will work closely with creators and internal teams to ensure that deliveries are executed efficiently, creatively, and in line with client expectations.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 Your Mission
   

  

**As An Influencer Marketing Manager, You Will**
* Lead the team’s delivery and be responsible for ensuring projects meet agreed budgets, timelines, and quality standards
* Receive and challenge client briefs to ensure they are realistic and feasible
* Ensure the team follows established processes and delivers according to deadlines
* Ensure influencer campaigns function as part of a broader media and brand strategy
* Coordinate projects, workflows, and processes to meet client expectations
* Collaborate with creators and ensure all deliverables maintain high quality
* Brief and support the team to enable effective project execution
* Use client-specific templates, documents, and reports correctly and professionally
* Ensure relevant client information is communicated to the team efficiently
* Participate in media meetings and build long-term relationships with both clients and internal client teams
* Follow up on campaigns, present results in a clear and educational manner, and implement learnings for future planning


**Qualifications**
 We Are Looking for Someone Who:
   

  

* Has 3 years of experience in marketing (PR, brand management, media buying, or similar), including at least 1 year within Influencer Marketing
* Is used to working in an international environment with stakeholders across different locations
* Has a strong interest in social media and influencer marketing
* Enjoys client interaction and can build long-term relationships
* Can manage internal and external stakeholders in an inspiring and professional manner
* Has the ability to inspire others and drive the team toward shared goals
* Is structured, self-driven, and accustomed to delivering high quality under pressure
* Has experience in project management and can coordinate multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously
* Understands how Influencer Marketing integrates into a holistic media and brand strategy


**Additional Information**
 In addition to a welcoming culture and inclusive environment with passionate colleagues we offer you:
   

  

**Career Opportunities**
 Local and Global opportunities through the Power Of One.
   

  

**Personal and Professional Learning**
 Global AI learning platform where you can participate in +30 000 classes and connect with colleagues from all over the world.
   

  

**Flexible Ways of Working**
 We want to be an employer where work life balance is cherished. We love to spend time with our colleagues at the office but also believe in the output of your work not where the work is being done.
   

  

**Work Your World**
 For six weeks a year, the world is yours. Every employee can work anywhere on the planet where there's a Publicis office. A new city, a favorite locale, or even a different country - Go. Grow. Explore.
   

  

**Mental Health**
 The happiness and health of our people is paramount to us. We work continuously on maintaining a healthy and safe working environment through initiatives that facilitate the well-being of our employees.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Generous benefit package.
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: Performance Marketing Manager
description:
At Qred, we’re building the bank for small businesses. Since launching 10 years ago, we’ve grown from startup to profitable fintech scale-up, now generating over 1 billion SEK in annual revenue and supporting 50,000+ entrepreneurs across Northern Europe. We combine smart technology, real data, and human judgment to make financing simple, fast, and fair. With bold growth plans and strong momentum across multiple markets, we’re now looking for a Performance Marketing Manager for the next phase of growth.
   

  

**About The Role**
 In this role, you will lead our digital acquisition strategy and mentor a team of specialists to drive high-quality growth across all markets. We are looking for a senior professional who can balance high-level strategic planning with a hands-on approach to execution. You will operate within our regulated Banking License environment, using attribution models through MMM to optimize our marketing spend. Your focus will be on scaling our reach while maintaining a disciplined approach to unit economics and sustainable growth.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Lead and mentor the performance marketing team to ensure professional growth and high output
* Develop and execute a multi-channel acquisition strategy across search, social, and display
* Oversee and optimize substantial marketing budgets to achieve ambitious growth targets
* Implement a structured testing and experimentation framework to improve conversion across the funnel
* Partner with cross-functional teams to align marketing efforts with overall business objectives
* Provide clear, data-driven reporting on channel performance and market trends
* Proficiency in GA4
* Experienced in working complex conversion event structure like offline conversions, enhanced conversions and restatement APIs


**What We’re Looking For**
 We are seeking an analytical leader with a background in Fintech or SaaS to drive long-term value and LTV/CAC efficiency in a fast-paced scale-up environment. You are a seasoned expert in Google, Meta, and LinkedIn Ads, with a proven track record of managing high-performing teams and translating complex data into strategic insights for senior stakeholders. Beyond immediate clicks, you focus on professional excellence, evidence-based decision-making, and navigating the full customer journey with total accountability.
   

  

**Qualifications**
* Experience: Several years in a similar role; Fintech experience is a plus.
* Leadership: Proven track record managing a team of 3–4 specialists.
* Scale: Experience managing annual budgets in the range of SEK 200M.
* Languages: Fluent in English; proficiency in an additional European language where Qred is active is a plus.


**Why Qred?**
 This is the place to be if you’re looking for a place to grow. Qred is growing fast, and our Qredsters along with it. With a non-bureaucratic organization and delegated responsibilities, we make sure there’s a short path from idea to action. In addition to our great culture, you get to work with the latest cutting-edge techniques, full ownership, and last but not least a bunch of great competent colleagues to learn from!
   

  

**One Last Thing**
 This is a full-time, permanent position based in our headquarters in Stockholm. While we encourage a hybrid setup, we do expect you to be at least three days per week at the office. We review applications on a rolling basis and while the start date is flexible, the right candidate can join us immediately.
   

  

 Qred celebrates diversity and does not discriminate based on ethnicity, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law.
   

  

  

  

 Qred is an international FinTech scale-up that specializes in digital financing solutions for small businesses, with operations in the Nordics, Benelux, Germany and Brazil. We are passionate about helping small businesses thrive and grow, by providing them with business loans and credit cards. Right now we’re in an exciting phase of our journey that focuses on growth through smart product development and technology fueled by international expansion.

title: (Senior) Project Manager in Procurement / Supply Chain Management
description:
At Inverto, we specialize in strategic procurement and supply chain management. We work globally with market-leading companies, developing transformational strategies and implementing them to make their businesses stronger. And you can too – with our continued success, this is your chance to grow as we grow. Our teams want to succeed – and you’ll be in great company, working with people and in a business that supports you to thrive. There’s never been a better time to join us as we develop and build our future together. Are you in?
   

  

**How You'll Impact.**
**Holistic project management.**
 You will be responsible for the development and successful implementation of suitable strategies to optimize the supply chain and strategic procurement process. You will demonstrate your entrepreneurial talent in project controlling and in the acquisition of new customers.
   

  

**Broad range of challenges.**
 Broad customer portfolios and project assignments at home and abroad for a wide variety of industries and customers. From start-ups, retail, and consumer goods, to the energy and automotive industries or the pharmaceutical and healthcare sectors.
   

  

**Intensive customer consulting.**
 You will work closely with customers, be their main contact person and be responsible for conducting your team's workshops, negotiations and presentations.
   

  

**Specific development.**
 Your professional expertise will help to drive forward the development of knowledge in our company.
   

  

**Far-reaching responsibility.**
 You will ensure that your team develops both professionally and personally. As a member of the Inverto management team, you can help shape the development of our entire company and give it the necessary impetus.
   

  

**What You'll Bring.**
**University degree with above-average grades** 
 and 4-8 years of relevant professional experience.
   

  

**Extensive expertise in consulting,** 
 ideally in procurement / supply chain management and a passion for strategic and operational work.
   

  

**You have already proven your analytical and conceptual skills** 
 at management level in demanding projects. You show full commitment, are customer-oriented and cooperative. You have shown that you can successfully lead teams professionally and personally.
   

  

**You thrive in a team** 
 , stay grounded, treat others with fairness, and bring authenticity to every interaction.
   

  

 Confident appearance in an international environment,
 **business fluent in English** 
 (any other Nordic language is a plus), flexible in your professional mobility.
   

  

 TOGETHER, WE BENEFIT.
   

  

* Tailored training programs and personalized coaching designed to help you grow and thrive
* Flexible working hours and travel options that support parents and promote work-life balance
* A comprehensive insurance package for your peace of mind
* Access to mental coaching to foster your well-being, resilience, and personal growth
* Individual mobility packages
* Seasonal parties, team events, and get-togethers to foster collaboration and celebrate success
* A rewarding Employee Referral Program that allows you to help grow our team


 APPLY NOW!

title: Regional BDR Team Manager
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.
   

  

 The company is founder led, profitable and growing. We are expanding our sales and field engineering teams globally, including our sales development representative (SDR) teams. Our SDRs are typically outstanding graduates from STEM fields who would like to join the software industry and are starting their sales career at Canonical.
   

  

 We are hiring
 **SDR Team Managers** 
 to run new inbound and outbound teams. Our goal is not only to manage inbound leads for a global tech company, but also to develop outstanding sales professionals with high integrity, high empathy and a rigorous work ethic. Our sales development organisation is therefor also sales academy, and as such, we are looking for SDR Team Managers who take a strong interest in the development of skills and the establishment of culture.
   

  

 You will enjoy this role if you are organised, persistent, analytical, hard-working, and care to ensure that smart and ambitious people learn and develop the skills they need to represent you and the company in outstandingly well over the course of their career - both while they are on your team, and as they move onward to more senior sales and alliances positions. You should be determined to grow your own skills as well as those of your team, and enjoy learning about new technologies. Canonical is fortunate to represent the vast wave of open source innovation, which means it has a large and rapidly growing range of solutions it can deliver to customers, and ample opportunity for all of us to learn about new kinds of tech, or specialise in fields we enjoy the most. You should also be open to innovation, and experiment with new outbound approaches to adapt to changes in the market. In this role your primary objective is to manage and mentor a successful team of 2-10 SDRs / BDRs, and maintain a multi-million dollar pipeline.
   

  

**We value:**
* Excellent academic results at school and university
* Bachelor's or equivalent degree in Business or STEM
* Knowledge and passion for business development, sales and technology
* Track record of bringing exceptional outbound sales development results
* Commitment to continuous learning and improvement - curious, flexible, scientific
* Creative problem solving and cross team collaboration
* Leadership experience of one form or another
* Able to use data to drive team activities and continuous improvement


 Over the last few years, this team has trained dozens of talented tech sales professionals who now work at Canonical and across the industry. Meet some of the SDRs who have graduated from our tech sales academy.
   

  

 What we offer you
   

  

 We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus or commission. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
   

  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events


**About Canonical**
 Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open-source projects and the platform for AI, IoT, and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence; in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since our inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
   

  

**Canonical is an equal opportunity employer**
 We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

title: Head of School - School of Mechanical & Manufacturing Engineering
description:
This Job is based in Australia
   

  

 The Faculty of Engineering at the University of New South Wales (UNSW) seeks a collaborative Head of School for the School of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering. Reporting to the Faculty Dean, you’ll shape the School’s vision, engage meaningfully with both internal and external stakeholders, and drive excellence in research, teaching, industry collaboration and societal impact. Your leadership in this role matters, as it will impact the next generation of engineers and addresses global challenges through cutting-edge research and education.
   

  

 This is a pivotal leadership role within the faculty to foster innovation and drive the strategic direction of a school recognised for its advancements in mechanical and manufacturing engineering. To qualify for this role, candidates must demonstrate a distinguished career in academia featuring significant research publications, teaching excellence, and leadership roles in an academic institution.
   

  

* Salary – Senior Academic Position
* Full time
* Fixed term – 5 years with an underlying continuing (permanent) Professorial appointment
* Location: Kensington – Sydney, Australia


**About The Faculty Of Engineering**
 At UNSW Engineering, we’re more than a collection of disciplines — we’re a community of innovators, creators, and problem‑solvers shaping a better future. Our culture is built on collaboration, curiosity, and a shared commitment to engineering solutions that matter.
   

  

* World‑Leading Impact: As one of the largest and most influential engineering faculties in Australia, we drive breakthroughs that transform industries, communities, and lives.
* Cutting‑Edge Research: From renewable energy and robotics to biomedical engineering and smart infrastructure, our research tackles the world’s most pressing challenges with bold, practical solutions.
* Industry Connection: We work hand‑in‑hand with global partners, giving our people access to real‑world projects, emerging technologies, and opportunities to shape the future of engineering practice.
* Innovative Learning Culture: Our environment encourages experimentation, creativity, and hands‑on learning. We empower students and staff to push boundaries and reimagine what’s possible.
* Women in Engineering: UNSW’s Women in Engineering program is a flagship initiative dedicated to increasing the participation, retention, and advancement of women in engineering. Through mentoring, leadership development, scholarships, outreach, and strong industry partnerships, we foster an environment where women can thrive and lead.
* Global Recognition: UNSW Engineering is consistently ranked among the top engineering schools worldwide, reflecting our commitment to excellence, innovation, and societal impact.
* Purpose‑Driven Careers: Whether you’re researching, teaching, or supporting our mission, you’ll be part of work that creates meaningful, lasting change — locally and globally.


**About The School Of Mechanical And Manufacturing Engineering**
 The School of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering at the UNSW opens doors to unparalleled innovation, transformative impact, and dynamic collaboration.
   

  

* Prestigious School: The School of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering at UNSW is renowned for its excellence in education and groundbreaking research.
* Innovative Research: Drive advancements in areas such as advanced manufacturing, robotics, aerospace, sustainable design, and mechanical systems.
* Global Impact: Contribute to a school whose research and innovations have reshaped industries worldwide and are frequently ranked among the best globally.
* State-of-the-Art Facilities: Access cutting-edge laboratories and facilities that empower world-class research and teaching.
* Collaborative Environment: Foster partnerships across academia, industry, and government to deliver impactful solutions.
* Student-Centered Excellence: The School is committed to nurturing future leaders through its innovative teaching methods and hands-on learning opportunities.
* Outstanding Reputation: Be part of a school known for producing graduates who lead and innovate in their fields globally.


**Why Your Role Matters**
 As the Head of School, you will influence the future of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering education, research, and industry collaboration. You will foster an inclusive and supportive culture shaping the academic environment, champion innovation, and drive impactful solutions. You will also play a pivotal role in developing staff and strengthening the overall capabilities of the school.
   

  

* Leadership: As Head of School, you’ll lead by example, fostering a culture of excellence, resilience, and vision.
* Engagement: Connect with industry partners, government bodies, and the community to drive impactful collaborations.
* Academic Development: Strengthen our capabilities in research, teaching, and industry engagement.
* Empowering Education: We go beyond teaching; we mentor. Our dedicated academics passionately share their extensive knowledge with graduate students, shaping the future of mechanical and manufacturing engineering.
* Pioneering Research: Step into a hub of innovation where global research teams and specialised centres push the boundaries of emerging technologies — from next‑generation sustainable materials to transformative advanced manufacturing processes.


**How To Apply**
 If you are interested in a Senior Leadership role that will shape the strategy and direction of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering at UNSW, please click the “Apply Now” button. Submit your CV, a cover letter, your responses to the skills and experience (found in the position description), and a vision statement (limited to 3 pages) outlining your plans for the next 2 and 5 years. We look forward to hearing from you!
   

  

 Additional details about the specific responsibilities for this position can be found in the position description. This is available via JOBS@UNSW.
   

  

 Please note that applications will not be accepted if sent to the contact listed below.
   

  

**Get In Touch**
 Eugene Aves – Talent Acquisition Consultant
   

  

 E: eugene.aves@unsw.edu.au
   

  

**Pre-Employment Checks**
 Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.
   

  

 These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:
   

  

* National and International Criminal history checks
* Entitlement to work and ID checks
* Working With Children Checks
* Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration
* Verification of relevant qualifications
* Verification of relevant professional membership
* Employment history and reference checks
* Financial responsibility assessments/checks.
* Medical Checks and Assessments


 Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW.
   

  

**Applications close:** 
 11:55 pm (Sydney time) on Sunday 12 April 2026
   

  

 UNSW is committed to evolving a culture that embraces equity and supports a diverse and inclusive community where everyone can participate fairly, in a safe and respectful environment. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage applications from people of diverse gender, sexual orientation, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people with disability and those with caring and family responsibilities. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options, including working from home, for eligible staff. UNSW provides 26 weeks of fully paid maternity or primary carers leave upon commencement of employment (subject to eligibility). UNSW is committed to enhancing the experience of ECAs by providing a supportive and collegial work environment and a dedicated framework to deliver support services to assist their research, teaching, and career development. The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.

title: Marketing Manager Biscuits & Meals
description:
**Job Description**
**Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International?**
**Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It With Pride.**
 Exciting opening to lead our bakery and meals team - great tasting categories if you ask us! And an opportunity to work with great tasting brands like Oreo, Marabou cookies and Philadelphia.
   

  

 The position as Marketing Manager Bakery & Meals is now available for someone who wants to join a fun and ambitious team working to make a difference. We are looking for someone with hunger not only for snacking but with a growth mindset for business as well as individually.
   

  

 You drive category strategy, future business development plans and objectives including new product development and platform opportunities. Leading the brand team and working with cross-functional and regional teams to implement a portfolio strategy that is aligned with the brand vision to drive sustainable growth.
   

  

**How You Will Contribute**
 You will unlock future-proof brand growth by developing and deploying marketing strategies grounded in deep consumer insight, data-driven decision making, and digital integration. The Marketing Manager Bakery & Meals will lead the brand team and the brand community to define and implement portfolio strategies, brand architecture, and brand-led innovation. This includes identifying white space opportunities, supporting category expansion, and creating compelling integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels.
   

  

 The role is responsible for driving brand excellence through both strategic and operational leadership. This includes leading local adaptation of global visual identity guidelines, overseeing creative agency collaboration, digital and media deployment to ensure effective activation across the consumer journey.
   

  

 The role collaborates closely with peers across Finland, Norway and Denmark, as well as with global category, insights, and innovation teams and sales team.
   

  

 Success in the role will be measured by brand growth and consumer engagement.
   

  

**What You Will Bring**
 A desire to drive your future and accelerate your career and the following experiences
   

  

* A proven track record leading marketing teams within branded consumer goods, with experience in both long-term brand strategy and in-market execution.
* Demonstrated ability to lead the development of brand architecture, portfolio strategy, and integrated marketing campaigns across multiple markets.
* Data driven and Analytical.
* Experience in brand-led product innovation, with a focus on consumer relevance and commercial impact (not R&D-led).
* Ability to manage cross-functional collaboration across insights, sales, media, and digital functions in a matrixed global organization.
* Solid understanding of digital marketing and eCommerce brand building, including digital media and partner management.


**What you need to know about this position:**
**Educational Background**
 A university degree in marketing, business, or a related field is preferred.
   

  

 Proven strategic marketing capabilities, leadership experience, and ability to deliver brand and business outcomes will be key.
   

  

**Job Specific Requirements**
 Experience of leading teams
   

  

**Travel Requirements**
 Limited 2-4 times/year
   

  

**Language**
 Full professional fluency in Swedish and English (written and spoken) is required.
   

  

**Work schedule**
 Fulltime based in Upplands Väsby office
   

  

**Preferred Experience (not a Requirement)**
 Familiarity with the Nordic market context and local consumer dynamics, particularly in Sweden.
   

  

 Exposure to fast-moving categories such as chocolate, dairy, or snacks is considered an advantage.
   

  

**Relocation Support Available?**
 No Relocation support available
   

  

**Business Unit Summary**
 We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!
   

  

*Our people make all the difference in our succes*
 Mondelēz International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
   

  

**Excited to grow your career?**
 We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!
   

  

**IF YOU REQUIRE SUPPORT TO COMPLETE YOUR APPLICATION OR DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS, PLEASE CONTACT THE RECRUITER**
**Job Type**
 Regular
   

  

 Brand & Portfolio Management
   

  

 Marketing

title: Senior Streaming Engineer
description:
We’re looking for a
 **Senior Streaming Engineer** 
 to join us.
   

  

**I’m Mikael Malmborg, Head of Video Streaming, and I’m searching for someone ready to grow with us.** 
 We’re a highly engaged team of experts within our domain focused on
 **building, securing, and scaling the streaming platform that delivers the award winning, world‑class Live and VOD Telia Play service to our Nordic customers** 
 .
   

  

 Within Video Streaming, our mission is simple but ambitious:
   

  

**We make everything behind the “play” button work when it comes to streaming - reliably, securely, and at scale.**
 In Streaming Infrastructure, we operate the backbone of that mission: the ingest, processing, packaging, delivery, observability, and automation that ensure our customers always get a smooth, high‑quality TV and streaming experience.
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
 As a Senior Streaming Engineer, you will together with the team
 **own and evolve our streaming media pipelines, platforms, and tooling that power the Telia Play streaming service across the Nordics.**
* Develop and operate high‑availability, automated streaming infrastructure across Live, VOD, and time‑shifted services.
* Create scalable DevOps workflows, observability capabilities, and tools that ensure reliability, security, and operational excellence end‑to‑end.
* Consult and collaborate with Product, Backend, Player, Network, and Security teams to drive technical strategy, best practices, and long‑term architectural direction.
* Lead improvements within Dynamic Ad Insertion (DAI), DRM, acquisition, encoding, streaming, anti-piracy, cloud scalability, CDN performance, and distributed systems reliability.


**What will help you thrive in this role:**
 You’ll thrive here if you enjoy solving large‑scale technical challenges, driving architectural clarity, and elevating engineering practices across team boundaries.
   

  

* Proven experience in streaming technologies, distributed systems, hardware, OS and cloud‑native DevOps engineering (5+ years).
* Strong knowledge of AWS, media services, DevOps toolchains, Dynamic Ad Insertion, DRM systems (Widevine, PlayReady, FairPlay), streaming protocols (HLS, DASH) and modern observability.
* Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands‑on problem‑solving, especially in high‑traffic, event‑driven environments.
* Fluency in English and comfort presenting complex technology clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.


**Important Information:**
* The role includes participating in an on‑call rotation approximately every 6-8 weeks.


**Join us to shape smarter living for people, businesses, and the planet**
 At Telia, we’re a team of 15 000 colleagues across the Nordics and Baltics, passionate about technology and connectivity. As a
 **Leading Telecommunications Operator** 
 &
 **Climate Leader** 
 , we drive change with large-scale, impactful projects while building a sustainable, inclusive world.
   

  

 Here, you can
 **grow your skills, lead meaningful projects, and thrive in a supportive, diverse environment.** 
 Whether you’re taking your first step or your next, Telia is a place to grow, belong, and make a real impact.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.
   

  

**For Lithuanian applicants only: monthly salary range for this position - 4300-6500 EUR (gross) + annual performance bonuses** 
 . Although it may vary depending on your skills and experience.
   

  

**Ready to make better happen?**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **April 25** 
**.**
 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner** 
 . Background checks will be part of the process.
   

  

**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by
 **professional skills and growth potential.** 
 We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where
 **your uniqueness is our strength.**
**Join us and let’s make better happen - together!**

title: Systemspecialist inom säkerhetsteknik till Swedavias flygplatser
description:
Swedavia söker fler systemspecialister som vill vara med och bidra till tryggheten och utvecklingen på våra flygplatser. Du blir en del av ett nytt team som har ansvar för att äga, utveckla och underhålla våra säkerhetslösningar, system som både skyddar människor och gör vår verksamhet möjlig. Skicka in din ansökan redan idag!
   

  

**Vad innebär rollen?**
 Som systemtekniker ansvarar du för felsökning, planerat underhåll och vidareutveckling av Swedavias teletekniska säkerhetssystem på flygplatserna, inklusive kameraövervakning och integrerade larm- och passagesystem. Du blir en del av ett växande team under uppbyggnad, där du får möjlighet att vara med och forma rutiner och arbetssätt.
   

  

 Arbetsuppgifter och ansvarsområden:
   

  

* Utföra planerat underhåll, inklusive uppdatering av applikationer och firmware
* Delta vid anläggningsprovningar
* Ansvara för felsökning och felavhjälpning
* Delta som resurs i projekt vid driftsättning av nya anläggningskomponenter (ny- och ombyggnationer)
* Säkerställa upprätthållande och uppdatering av anläggningsdata, exempelvis larmtexter och grafik
* Samarbeta med andra discipliner inom organisationen för att uppnå önskad funktionalitet
* Uppdatera och underhålla teleteknisk dokumentation
* Bidra med konsultativt stöd internt


 I tjänsten förekommer beredskap för att säkerställa systemen fungerar dygnet runt.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
 Du trivs med komplexa problem och utmaningar, och har en naturlig förmåga att hitta lösningar. Du kommunicerar effektivt på både engelska och svenska och förstår vikten av strukturerade tidsplaner för att leverera i tid. Du tar initiativ, är flexibel och samarbetar gärna med kollegor och andra aktörer. Vi ser även att du har:
   

  

* Minst fem års dokumenterad arbetslivserfarenhet inom teletekniska säkerhetssystem
* Relevant gymnasie- eller yrkeshögskoleutbildning inom IT, teknik eller el, alternativt motsvarande kompetens genom arbetslivserfarenhet
* Flerårig dokumenterad erfarenhet av att arbeta i systemen TTLabWest Sentrion Unision och Bosch VMS
* Erfarenhet inom driftsättning av säkerhetssystemens (Sentrion Unison och Bosh VMS) hårdvara samt avancerad programmering och konfiguration i tillhörande systemapplikationer
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att arbeta med avancerad systemadministration samt felsökning och felavhjälpning i komplexa systemmiljöer
* Goda kunskaper i SQL
* B-körkort, tillgång till bil samt möjlighet att ta sig till flygplatsen inom en timme vid beredskap


 Vi ser det som meriterande om du har:
   

  

* Utbildning eller certifiering inom Pacom Unison med Sentrion
* BVMS Expert Certification eller motsvarande utbildning inom Bosch VMS
* Utbildning inom IP-kamerasystem och transmissionsutrustning


**Vad erbjuder vi dig?**
 Vi erbjuder dig en omväxlande och glad arbetsplats i ett team med stora möjligheter att påverka. Visste du att på Swedavia får du bidrag till din arbetsresa om du åker kollektivt, elbil, cykel eller tillsammans med en kollega? Du har också möjlighet att semesterväxla och byta bort din semesterersättning mot lediga dagar. Du kan läsa mer om våra förmåner på Förmåner | Om Swedavia .
   

  

 Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta jan-erik.pettersson@swedavia.se.
   

  

**Sista ansökningsdag är den 10/5, urval sker löpande och annonsen kan stängas tidigare så tveka inte att söka redan idag!**
**Hur är det att arbeta på Swedavia?**
 Vårt fokus är att möjliggöra hållbart resande och uppmuntra nytänkande för att skapa en välkomnande, trygg och inkluderande arbetsgemenskap där du kan växa och göra verklig skillnad – läs mer här! Jobba hos oss | Om Swedavia
   

  

 Då flygplatser är en säkerhetsklassad verksamhet genomgår samtliga kandidater som går vidare i rekryteringsprocessen en bakgrundskontroll. För vissa tjänster även en säkerhetsprövning. Swedavia är även en drogfri arbetsplats med nolltolerans mot droger. Drogtester genomförs före anställning och sker slumpmässigt under anställningen.

title: Key Account Manager
description:
Ready to take full ownership of the sales cycle and make a real impact within the public sector? Join Dustin and be part of a collaborative, high-performing team where your ideas, growth, and success truly matter.
   

  

**This is what makes the position as Key Account Manager unique**
* Own the full sales cycle and shape long-term account strategies with real impact on both customer success and business growth
* Join a collaborative, knowledge-driven team where expertise is shared openly and wins are achieved together
* Work closely with experienced colleagues and a supportive leader who actively invests in your professional development and success


 My name is Erik Markusson. I am the Sales Director. As a leader, I strive to create an engaging, results-driven, and supportive environment where you feel empowered to grow, take ownership, and truly make an impact.
   

  

**Your role & influence as Key Account Manager**
*Responsibility* 
 : You will be given responsibility for building trust-based relationships with public sector customers and positioning Dustin as their preferred IT partner by driving tailored solution sales from prospecting to close.
   

  

*Collaboration* 
 : Together with your team, you take care of winning tenders and developing strong relationships with public sector customers. You'll work closely not only with your immediate team members but also with departments such as Product & Services Specialists and Sales Support.
   

  

*Core activities:*
* Proactively develop business with both existing and new public sector customers, identifying opportunities and building long-term relationships
* Own and manage the full sales cycle from prospecting and tendering to negotiation and closing deals
* Create and execute strategic account plans to drive growth, deliver on targets, and position Dustin as a trusted IT partner


**Benefits Of Joining Dustin**
 We believe there's more to Dustin than just the benefits listed here. You'll fully understand this once you're with us. Curious? Our Life at Dustin - Dustin Sweden page has you covered.
   

  

 For this role specific you can expect:
   

  

* A quarterly bonus paid upon achieving your targets
* Lease car


**You can find more of our benefits below in the vacancy.**
**This Is What We Imagine You Bring**
 We envision you as a curious and driven individual who thrives in a proactive sales environment, builds strong relationships, and is motivated to create long-term value for customers.
   

  

*Additionally, it's important that:*
* You have a bachelor's degree level of working and thinking
* Experience with B2B sales, preferably towards the public sector
* Experience or knowledge of public tendering processes and a strong network within the Swedish public sector is a plus
* Technical skills and understanding of IT solutions, enabling you to translate customer needs into tailored offerings


**What can you expect next?**
 To ensure a fair recruitment process free from unconscious bias, we handle your application anonymously and do not accept cover letters. Please refrain from sending one, so that the selection is based solely on professional competencies. We expect the following steps in the recruitment process:
   

  

* Telephone screening with TA Partner Ida Wannebro
* 1st interview with Director Public Erik Markusson
* Assessment (Personality- and cognitive thinking test)
* 2nd interview with Erik and someone from the team
* References

title: Geo Hunter Enterprise Account Executive (Nordics)
description:
SLSQ327R80
   

  

 Want to help solve the world's toughest problems with data and AI? This is what we do every day at Databricks.
   

  

 Databricks operates at the leading edge of the Data and AI space. Our customers rely on us to drive the accelerated innovation needed to stay ahead in today’s highly competitive landscape.
   

  

 We are looking for a Geo Enterprise Hunter Account Executive to join the team to maximise the phenomenal market opportunity that exists for Databricks across the Nordics. Reporting to the Director of Enterprise Sales, you will be responsible for driving strategic growth and expanding our footprint across the region’s enterprise accounts. This role can be based in Stockholm, Sweden, or Denmark.
   

  

**The Impact You Will Have**
* Assess your list of assigned accounts and develop a strategy to execute against it
* Build a customer base, identifying and closing new logo opportunities
* Lead your customers and partners to identify impactful big data and AI use cases whilst proving out their value on the Databricks Lakehouse platform.
* Identify land opportunities and develop the expand / consumption use cases.
* Orchestrate and utilise internal teams to maximise the impact on your ecosystem.
* Build exceptional value with all engagements to guide successful negotiations to close point


**What We Look For**
* Experience selling SaaS solutions to Enterprise Customers
* Extensive experience and strong performance in new logo hunting
* Experience working in Big Data, Cloud, or SaaS industries with some technical sales experience
* Successful experience selling complex software deals and quota over-achievement
* Understanding of consumption based land and expand sales models is advantageous
* Knowledge of the Data & AI space with some technology sales experience
* Evidence of creating demand that delivered substantial business value
