* Experience of following robust sales methodologies and process, e.g. account planning, MEDDPICC, Value Selling and accurate forecasting
* Experience of building effective champions, collaborative teams and partnerships to support execution of your territory plan.
* Understanding of how to identify all key uses cases and buying centres in an opportunity to increase the impact of Databricks in an organisation
* Fluency in business English and a Nordic language is required


**About Databricks**
 Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region, please visit https://www.mybenefitsnow.com/databricks.
   

  

**Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
   

  

**Compliance**
**If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.**

title: Systems Consultant – Oracle HC till Dovista
description:
If you have experience working with enterprise HR/ERP systems, enjoy solving complex data and system challenges, and want to play a key role in a large-scale HR transformation – we would love to hear from you!
   

  

 DOVISTA is now looking for a Systems Consultant – Oracle HCM to join the Digital Business Solutions team. In this role, you will be a key contributor in the implementation of Oracle HCM and take long-term responsibility for configuring, supporting, and optimizing the system in a global organization.
   

  

**Your daily work**
 As a Systems Consultant for Oracle HCM, you will support the initial implementation and then take ownership of the system’s ongoing development, configuration, and stability. The role combines strategic responsibility with hands-on technical work, where data, integrations, and system logic are central.
   

  

 You will work closely with HR, IT, and external vendors across multiple countries, ensuring high data quality, stable integrations, and continuous system improvements. After go-live, you will play a key role in upgrades, releases, testing, and optimization of the Oracle HCM platform.
   

  

 Your responsibilities include
   

  

* Configure and maintain Oracle HCM modules in line with business and HR requirements
* Troubleshoot system and data-related issues, including performance and data consistency
* Develop, optimize, and maintain SQL queries, reports, and data extracts
* Support and monitor integrations between Oracle HCM and surrounding systems
* Act as a key driver for system improvements, collaborating closely with HR, IT, and external vendors
* Support system upgrades, manage testing cycles, and coordinate releases
* Ensure high data quality, governance, and structured documentation
* Provide support to local and international stakeholders, ensuring efficient issue resolution


 This is what you will contribute with
   

  

* Experience working with large enterprise HR/ERP systems (e.g. Oracle HCM, Workday, SAP, Dynamics)
* Strong SQL skills and solid understanding of relational databases and data structures
* Experience with HR/ERP reporting and data extraction tools
* Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
* Good understanding of HR processes and how they translate into system logic
* Ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders


**Preferred Experience & Qualifications**
* Hands-on experience with Oracle HCM (Cloud or E-Business Suite)
* Experience from global HR/ERP implementations in international organizations
* Experience with integrations (APIs, file-based loads, data flows)
* Knowledge of Oracle reporting tools such as OTBI or BI Publisher
* Experience with data migration or data quality initiatives


**Who are you?**
 You are analytical, structured, and quality-focused, with a strong service mindset. You enjoy working at the intersection of HR, IT, and data, and you take pride in creating stable, scalable, and well-functioning system solutions. You are proactive, collaborative, and comfortable taking ownership in a complex system landscape.
   

  

**About DOVISTA**
 DOVISTA Group is the leading supplier of facade windows and exterior doors in Europe, with approximately 7 000 employees across 10 European countries. We are owned by the VKR Group – a Danish family- and foundation-owned company with around 17,500 employees worldwide.
   

  

 In Sweden, we operate under the brands Svenska Fönster, Mockfjärds, and VELFAC, with around 600 employees. We offer a collaborative and development-oriented culture, driven by continuous improvement and long-term sustainability.
   

  

 Location: Solna, Sweden (Hybrid)
   

  

**Contact**
 In this recruitment, DOVISTA collaborates with Wise IT. For more information, please contact Recruitment Consultant Sandra Åhs at sandra.ahs@wiseit.se.
   

  

 Please note: We are unable to accept applications via email due to GDPR regulations. All applications must be submitted through the official application system.
   

  

 We review applications continuously and conduct interviews on a rolling basis.
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: Senior/Staff Machine Learning Engineer - Bidding (Bangkok based, relocation provided)
description:
**About Agoda**
 At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.
   

  

 Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.
   

  

 No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.
   

  

**Get to Know Our Team**
 The Engineering department oversees all Agoda’s ML and software related requirements. Our goal is to enable and increase Agoda’s business through creative approaches and the implementation of powerful resources such as operational and analytical databases, ML driven solutions, queue systems and data monitoring tools. We hire the brightest minds from around the world to take on this challenge and equip them with the knowledge and tools that contribute to their personal growth and success while supporting our company’s culture of diversity and experimentation. The role the engineering team plays at Agoda is critical as business users, product managers, and many others rely on us to empower their decision making. We are equally dedicated to our customers by improving their search experience with faster results and protecting them from any fraudulent activities. Data is interesting only when you have enough of it, and we have plenty. This is what drives up the challenge as part of the ML engineering squad, but also the reward. We work across structured and non-structured data at scale. For example, our current ML models process millions of images every day to try and enhance the experience that our users get from our app.
   

  

**Why Agoda Engineering Team (ML role)?**
 Our engineering teams are at the intersection of business analytics, ml engineering, data warehousing and software engineering. Our job involves dealing with distributed systems, stream processing, ml modeling and computation at tens of PB Scale. We focus on software engineering related to data replication, storage, centralized computation, and Data API’s. By providing our users with ML products/tools, shared frameworks, and ML services, we enable our company to validate strategic decisions, make smarter choices, and react to the fast-changing world. We are a small but passionate team with people from different nationalities working together on a single goal.
   

  

**In this Role, you will get to:**
* Lead the team technically in improving scalability, stability, accuracy, speed and efficiency of our existing ML systems and processes. Build, administer and scale ML processing pipelines.
* Be comfortable navigating the following technology stack: Python3, Scala Spark, scripting (Bash/Python), Hadoop, SQL, S3 etc.
* Should be able to understand internals of ML models such as LightGBM, Random Forest, CNN, Regression models, etc
* Design, build, test and deploy new libraries, frameworks or full systems for our core systems while keeping to the highest standards of testing and code quality.
* Work with experienced engineers and product owners to identify and build tools to automate many large-scale data management / analysis tasks.
* We believe in end-to-end ownership; this role will involve taking and building systems that enable us to deliver ML models to production at a scale.


**What You’ll need to Succeed:**
* Bachelor’s degree in computer science /information systems/engineering/related field 6+ years of experience in software engineering with a minimum of 4+ years in ML
* Expert in SQL, Python3, and JVM based programming language – ie, Java or Scala
* Expert in software design/design patterns, functional programming and object-oriented programming.
* Analytical problem-solving capabilities & experience. A strong engineering driven mindset will be required to succeed in this role.


**It’s great if you have:**
* Good understanding of Hadoop ecosystems and Big Data architecture and techologies
* Experience working with Open-source products
* Experience with Scala development
* Working in an agile environment using test driven methodologies
* Systems administration skills in Linux


**Benefits**
* Hybrid Working Model
* WFH Set Up Allowance
* 30 Days of Remote Working from anywhere globally every year
* Employee discount for accommodation globally
* Global team of 90+ nationalities
* 40+ offices and 25+ countries
* Annual CSR / Volunteer Time off
* Benevity Subscription for employee donations
* Volunteering opportunities globally
* Free Headspace subscription
* Free Odilo & Udemy subscriptions
* Access to Employee Assistance Program (third party for personal and workplace support)
* Enhanced Parental Leave
* Life, TPD & Accident Insurance

title: MLRO to Resurs Bank
description:
*Are you an experienced AML specialist who wants to combine deep expertise with real influence in a changing business? Resurs Bank is now looking for an MLRO to take on a key role in leading, developing, and strengthening the bank’s AML/CFT work.* 




  





**About the role** 




 The role is fully dedicated to MLRO responsibilities and comes with a clear mandate to strengthen structure, methodology, and ways of working across the area. Responsibilities include all the Bank’s countries of operation. You will be part of the compliance function and work closely with the Head of Compliance, your compliance colleagues, the Financial Crime Prevention team and the business. In this position, you will play a central role in shaping how the bank works with AML/CFT going forward, ensuring that the framework is robust, business-anchored, and aligned with both regulatory expectations and organisational needs.
 



 Your responsibilities will include:
 



  




* monitor and regularly control compliance with AML/CFT legislation in the Nordic countries where the Bank operates as well as with internal rules and regulations
* assessing and following up on AML/CFT issues
* ensuring reporting of suspicious activities and transactions and answering to requests from the FIUs
* reporting to the CEO and the Board
* monitoring regulatory developments and assessing their impact for the Bank
* providing qualified support to the business in matters relating to new products, process changes, and system improvements as well as other implications for the business of AML/CFT rules
* audit, monitor and control internal procedures, guidelines and AML/CFT measures in place and assess whether they are compliant, appropriate and effective and provide recommendations based on findings
* General AML/CFT training



  





 This is a role for someone who enjoys combining specialist depth with influence in the business. You will have the opportunity to work close to decision-making, contribute to a strategically important area, and play a meaningful part in the bank’s continued development.
 



 As the regulatory landscape continues to evolve, not least through the AML Package, this role also offers scope for someone who is adaptable, forward-looking, and motivated by helping shape an area that will continue to change over time.
 



  





**About you** 




 We are looking for a senior AML professional with strong hands-on experience and the confidence to take independent ownership of a specialist area. To succeed in this role, we believe you bring:
 



  




* at least five years of relevant AML experience
* very strong experience from the second line of defence
* experience from a bank, payment institution, fintech company, or another regulatory complex business
* fluency in Swedish and English
* a law degree or other relevant academic background



  





 A law degree is an advantage, but not a requirement. What matters most is your ability to interpret and apply regulations, technical standards, and legal frameworks in practice.
 



 You are confident, self-driven, and comfortable making independent assessments. At the same time, you are structured, pragmatic, and able to move things forward in a changing business environment. We also believe that you are curious, forward-looking, and motivated by improving ways of working. Experience in developing and delivering training is a requirement, and an interest in technology, AI, and new ways of working is a clear advantage.
 



  





**Location and ways of working** 




 The role is based in Helsingborg or Stockholm. For those based in Stockholm, travel is a natural part of the role, especially at the beginning, in order to build presence in the business and establish strong relationships.
 



  





**Resurs in brief** 




 At Resurs, we make everyday finances easier through innovative and customer-focused solutions. With customers across the Nordics and more than 800 colleagues, we are committed to creating long-term value for individuals, businesses, and society. Recognized as a career company for the past six years, we put strong emphasis on both personal and professional growth. We want to make a difference by striving to be our best, acting with kindness, and building trust through clarity and collaboration. Our heart beats strong and it shows in everything we do. Although we are based in the Nordics, our team is enriched by the breadth of perspectives brought by colleagues from different countries. We foster a welcoming and supportive culture, where collaboration drives us to achieve the best outcomes for our users.
 



  





**Questions and Application** 




 In this recruitment, we are collaborating with Sharp Recruitment & Consultants. For questions, please contact
 



 Peter Ekström
 



 070 813 51 86 | peter.ekstrom@sharprecrutiment.se
 



 Your application should consist of a resume and a cover letter. The start date will be agreed upon, and selection is ongoing, so please apply today.

title: Territory Manager, Endoscopy (Visualization and Communication) - Northern Sweden
description:
**Visualization & Communication (Endoscopy)**
**Northern Sweden**
 At
 **Stryker** 
 , we are driven to make healthcare better.
   

  

 We are now looking for a commercially strong, structured and ambitious
   

  

 Territory Manager to take ownership of our Visualization & Communication portfolio in Northern / Southern Sweden. This is a high-impact role with full territory responsibility in a market that rewards resilience, strategic thinking and strong relationship-building.
   

  

**Visualization & Communication portfolio:** 
 Endoscopy | Stryker
   

  

**Your Mission**
 You will drive sales and market development of our Visualization & Communication portfolio across your territory. From operating theatres to procurement offices, from clinical workshops to contract negotiations you will own the full sales cycle.
   

  

**What You Will Be Responsible For**
* Taking full ownership of your territory and sales targets.
* Building and maintaining strong relationships with surgeons, theatre staff, hospital administration and procurement.
* Driving and managing complex tender processes (framework and volume-based agreements).
* Implementing marketing and sales strategies aligned with Nordic objectives.
* Conducting impactful product demonstrations in OR settings and clinical workshops.
* Advising and training surgical staff on product use.
* Preparing and managing orders, ensuring seamless follow-up and high customer satisfaction.
* Representing Stryker at congresses, trade fairs and professional meetings to expand your network and identify new business opportunities.


 You will start with an intensive onboarding and product training phase to ensure you are fully equipped to succeed.
   

  

**The Profile We Are Looking For**
 This role requires more than enthusiasm for the OR. It demands commercial maturity and structure.
   

  

 You bring:
   

  

* 4+ years of sales experience within medical devices, pharmaceutical or a comparable healthcare industry.
* Strong understanding of the Swedish healthcare landscape.
* Experience working in tender-driven environments (highly preferred).
* A University degree in medical technology, health management, business, science or a technical field.
* A passion for selling complex products and delivering high-level customer service.
* The ability to balance relationship-building with disciplined administrative execution.
* A valid driving license.
* Fluent Swedish and strong English skills (written and spoken).


 Endoscopy experience is an advantage, but a proven sales mindset in healthcare is more critical.
   

  

**What Makes This Role Unique**
* Full ownership of a strategically important territory.
* Opportunity to grow a market with strong future potential.
* Exposure to key opinion leaders and hospital decision-makers.
* A structured, world-class product portfolio with strong brand recognition.
* A competitive compensation package including base salary and commission.


 Stryker is a global leader in medical technologies and, together with its customers, is driven to make healthcare better. The company offers innovative products and services in MedSurg, Neurotechnology, Orthopaedics and Spine that help improve patient and healthcare outcomes. Alongside its customers around the world, Stryker impacts more than 150 million patients annually.

title: Intelligent Automation – Senior Business Analyst, Pega
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 CGI's practice inom intelligent automation växer och vi letar efter seniora BAs med stark domänkunskap inom bank (gärna lending) som kommer ingå i vår Financial Services enhet i Stockholm. Vi är många som är specialiserade på Pega plattformen och vi utvärderar kontinuerligt marknaden.
   

  

 Kom och bli en del av ett växande team av experter med olika bakgrunder och branschkunskap. Om du är en senior konsult som vill fortsätta karriären i ett stort globalt bolag med trygghet och en stark lokal förankring är vi rätt för dig.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Arbetet på CGI omfattas av en dynamisk arbetsmiljö och en spännande vardag. Vi erbjuder dig möjligheten att utvecklas som konsult både i lokala kundleveranser och i större globala åtaganden. Det finns goda möjligheter till individuell kompetensutveckling, vilket gör att du kontinuerligt kan bidra värdefullt både till dina egna ansvarsområden och till projektets helhet.
   

  

 Du kan komma att arbeta i kortare fokuserade projekt eller i större, långsiktiga åtaganden. Vi deltar i och driver projekt både på plats hos kund och från våra lokala kontor. Vi arbetar både individuellt och i team tillsammans med kollegor. Våra uppdrag genomförs med stöd av etablerade metoder och modeller baserade på branschstandarder.
   

  

 Vi bygger framtidens ledare - och vi har roligt på vägen!
   

  

 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 Vi söker seniora experter med fokus på intelligent automation och erfarenhet inom bank, gärna inom Lending. Pega Business Architect certifiering är inte ett krav men vi förväntar oss att du vill lära dig plattformen och ta nödvändiga certifikat. Vi premierar konsulter som redan bor och arbetar i Stockholm då det är här våra kunder finns.
   

  

 För denna roll prioriterar vi kandidater som behärskar svenska flytande i tal och skrift, samt har en akademisk examen och relevant arbetslivserfarenhet. Vi förväntar oss att du har minst 8+ års erfarenhet som business analyst eller business architect. Du trivs i rollen mellan verksamhet och IT och känner dig trygg i att driva kravarbete i både stora och små organisationer.
   

  

 Du deltar och driver utformningen av färdplan och riktning för att bygga framtidens betalningslösningar och/eller lånaaffären. Självklart samarbetar du med arkitekter och leveransledare för att driva strategiska lösningar i samband med pågående projektleveranser, vilket kräver stark drivkraft, diplomati och pragmatism. Du kan hantera och leverera både funktionella och icke funktionella krav.
   

  

 Du är skicklig på att planera och genomföra resultatinriktade workshops och kan både muntligt och visuellt fånga krav samt leverera ditt underlag i tid. Vi förväntar oss att du kan driva nya initiativ från start till mål utifrån ett kravperspektiv och att du är van att jobba agilt samt kunna hantera olika typer av stakeholders hos kunden.
   

  

**Vill du veta mer?**
 Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig! Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande så skicka in din ansökan redan i dag! Om du har frågor om rollen, hör gärna av dig till rekryterande ledare Carl Ahlin, Director Consulting Services/ Konsultchef, på ca.larsson@cgi.com
   

  

**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Rättssakkunnig till Finansdepartementet
description:
**Avdelningen för bolag med statligt ägande**
 Är du assessor eller kvalificerad jurist med ett intresse för att arbeta i en politisk miljö med omfattande interna och externa kontakter? Är du dessutom samhällsintresserad och har ett intresse för bolagsfrågor? I så fall kan detta uppdrag vara intressant för dig.
   

  

**Din arbetsdag. Sveriges morgondag.**
 Avdelningen för bolag med statligt ägande inom Finansdepartementet förvaltar ca 40 bolag, bl.a. Vattenfall, LKAB, Telia Company, Apoteket och Systembolaget. Den statligt ägda bolagsportföljen sysselsätter ca 122 000 personer och är värderad till över 850 miljarder kronor. Avdelningen för bolag med statligt ägande har ca 30 anställda med experter inom områdena juridik, bolagsförvaltning, bolagsanalys, hållbart företagande och styrelserekrytering.
   

  

 I den roll vi nu tillsätter inom gruppen bolagsstyrning söker vi en hovrätts- eller kammarrättsassessor, eller kvalificerad jurist med erfarenhet av författningsarbete. Vi söker en självständig och lösningsorienterad person med intresse för bolagsfrågor, gärna med kompetens eller erfarenhet inom bolagsrätt, statsstöd, bank- och finans-, och/eller sjörätt.
   

  

 I arbetsuppgifterna ingår att göra rättsutredningar och utarbeta departementspromemorior, lagrådsremisser, propositioner och författningar rörande aktuella frågor inom ramen för förvaltningen av den statligt ägda bolagsportföljen. Ett annat vanligt inslag i vardagen är att med kort varsel ta fram olika typer av faktabaserade underlag till den politiska ledningen i form av promemorior och underlag avseende legala frågeställningar inom ramen för bolagsförvaltningen. Arbetet kräver att du har ett genuint intresse för samhällsfrågor och bolagsrätt samt att du snabbt kan sätta dig in i nya frågor och driva processer framåt för att uppnå resultat.
   

  

 Du kommer att arbeta tillsammans med professionella och kunniga kollegor, både specialister och personer med bred erfarenhet. Rollen innebär många kontaktytor och samarbete med andra avdelningar i Finansdepartementet. Du kommer även att ha kontaktmed övriga departement inom Regeringskansliet samt kontakter med företrädare för statligt ägda företag.
   

  

 Vi har ett digitalt arbetssätt, vilket ger förutsättningar att delvis arbeta hemifrån.
   

  

**Läs mer om vår verksamhet och** 
**lyssna på våra medarbetare berätta hur det är att arbeta som rättssakkunnig på RK:** 
**Jobba som rättssakkunnig - Regeringen.se**
**Din bakgrund** 
 Vi söker dig som har juristexamen. Du är hovrätts- eller kammarrättsassessor, eller kvalificerad jurist med erfarenhet av författningsarbete. Du har flera års aktuell erfarenhet av kvalificerat juridiskt arbete, du är en skicklig skribent och har vana av att skriva mycket. Du är även stringent föredragande med en utvecklad förmåga att anpassa föredragningar till olika målgrupper och lyfta fram det mest väsentliga i muntliga sammanhang. Vidare har du mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska.
   

  

 Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av politiskt styrda beslutsprocesser och ärendehantering inom myndighet. Det är även meriterande med goda kunskaper inom följande områden:
   

  

* bolags-/associationsrätt
* finansiella regelverk
* statsstöd, offentlig upphandling och konkurrensrätt
* sjörätt
* regelverk av relevans för totalförsvaret.


**Dina egenskaper**
 Som person är du driven och lösningsinriktad samt analytisk, strukturerad och kvalitetsmedveten. Vidare har du gott omdöme och kan arbeta självständigt.
   

  

 Regeringskansliets medarbetarkriterier är helhet, utveckling och resultat. Alla som arbetar i Regeringskansliet behöver ha en helhetssyn och samarbeta för verksamhetens bästa. Du förväntas bidra med ditt perspektiv och din kompetens samt vara öppen för andras. Du kommer med idéer och nya angreppssätt för att förbättra och utveckla verksamheten samt din egen kompetens. Du är inriktad på resultat i ditt agerande och anpassar dig när behov och riktning ändras.
   

  

 Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.
   

  

**Övrigt**
 Vi erbjuder en tidsbegränsad anställning på ca ett år med tillträde snarast. Arbetsorten är i första hand Stockholm men det kan även finnas möjlighet till distansarbete med regelbundna resor till kontoret i Stockholm. Tjänsten är förlagd på heltid och säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll genomförs innan beslut om anställning. Du måste vara svensk medborgare, men får gärna ha utländsk bakgrund.
   

  

**Är du intresserad?** 
 Vill du veta mer om jobbet och vad vi erbjuder, kontakta bitr. avdelningschef Gustaf Hygrell eller rekryteringsspecialist Marjut Thelin. Fackliga kontaktpersoner är Simon Monti Danielsson och Miriam Ben Hadj för Saco samt Lotta Nordqvist för ST. Samtliga nås via Regeringskansliets växel, 08-4051000. Vi tar gärna emot samtal från dig som är intresserad av jobbet men vi tackar nej till dig som säljer annonser och rekryteringstjänster.
   

  

 Välkommen att registrera din ansökan i vårt rekryteringsverktyg den 22 april, 2026.
 **Bifoga samtliga betyg och vitsord med din ansökan.**
 Regeringskansliets uppdrag är att stödja regeringen i dess arbete med att styra Sverige och förverkliga sin politik. Här arbetar 4 800 personer, varav 600 utomlands. Regeringskansliet välkomnar sökande med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter. Vi ser jämställdhet och mångfald som en styrka och tillgång och vi arbetar aktivt för att vara en arbetsplats fri från diskriminering. Finansdepartementet ansvarar för frågor som rör statens ekonomi. Det handlar om samordning av statens budget, prognoser och analyser, skattefrågor samt styrning och förvaltning av statens verksamhet. Dessutom ansvarar departementet för frågor som rör finansmarknad, kommuner och regioner, upphandling, bolag med statligt ägande, digitalisering och it samt konsumentlagstiftning.

title: Senior Tax Specialist – Global Tax & Transfer Pricing
description:
**Job Description**

 Are you ready to make global impact? We’re looking for a Senior Tax Specialist to join our Global Tax & Transfer Pricing team. In this international role, you’ll support operations across Direct Tax, VAT, selected Transfer Pricing areas, and OECD Pillar 2 reporting.
   

  





 You’ll join a close-knit global team of eight, recognised for structured ways of working and certified tax assurance processes. This is a great opportunity to strengthen our in-house capabilities while contributing to a global framework in a fast-moving, international environment.
   

  





**WHAT YOU’LL DO**




 As a Senior Tax Specialist, you will strengthen our in-house tax capabilities by supporting global compliance, reporting and advisory topics across markets. You will also help drive continuous improvement and digitalisation of the way we work, ensuring strategic alignment across the organisation.
   

  





**Key responsibilities include:**



* Support global Transfer Pricing documentation and compliance
* Contribute to implementing and maintaining OECD Pillar 2 compliance at country level
* Ensure accurate and timely Direct Tax compliance and selected Indirect Tax compliance, in close collaboration with internal teams and external partners
* Act as a first-line advisor to the business on corporate tax and transfer pricing topics, including withholding tax, VAT, customs valuation and intra-group agreements
* Support tax audits and manage communication with tax advisors and authorities
* Drive continuous improvement and digitalisation of tax processes and ways of working



**WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH**




 You will report to the Global Head of Tax and be part of a cross-functional set-up driving our global tax framework. You’ll work closely with Finance, Legal and business stakeholders across markets, as well as external advisors and tax authorities when needed. With a small but global team, you’ll have broad exposure and strong collaboration across countries and functions.
   

  





**WHO YOU ARE**




 You’re a structured and collaborative professional who enjoys combining technical expertise with business impact. You can navigate complex topics, communicate clearly and confidently, and you’re comfortable taking ownership in a global matrix organization.
   

  





 You have a university degree in Tax, Accounting, Finance or similar, and at least five years of experience in corporate tax and/or transfer pricing, ideally in a multinational commercial environment. You are comfortable working in SAP, and you have the confidence to “sell” and “defend” transfer pricing transactions and set-ups in direct contact with tax authorities. Experience or knowledge of OECD Pillar 2 compliance is a strong plus. You are fluent in English, both written and spoken, and you feel confident in formal discussions and negotiations.
   

  





**WHO WE ARE**




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. Read more about us here
   

  





**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE**




 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe.
   

  





 An example of our benefits:
   

  




* 25% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands, both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories and ARKET).


* H&M Incentive Program – HIP. Learn more about the program here.



**JOIN US** 





 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.
   

  





 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.
   

  





 This is a
 **permanent position** 
 based in Stockholm, please send your CV in English as soon as possible.
 *(For internal candidates, please initiate a dialogue with you manager before applying).* 
  

  





*\*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.*

title: Kravanalytiker – konsultuppdrag inom digital utveckling
description:
Vi söker nu en kravanalytiker till ett konsultuppdrag där du kommer att arbeta med både befintliga system och nya initiativ inom digital utveckling. Uppdraget passar dig som trivs i gränslandet mellan verksamhet och IT och har förmåga att omsätta behov till tydliga och strukturerade krav.

**Om uppdraget**  

I rollen ansvarar du för att utveckla, anpassa och förvalta kravdokumentation för IT-lösningar, med fokus på att möta både nuvarande och framtida verksamhetsbehov. Du arbetar nära såväl verksamhet som utvecklingsteam och bidrar till att säkerställa att lösningarna är relevanta, genomförbara och väl förankrade.


Arbetet omfattar hela kravprocessen – från insamling och analys till dokumentation, prioritering och uppföljning av både funktionella och icke-funktionella krav.

**Arbetsuppgifter inkluderar:**

* Samla in, analysera och dokumentera krav
* Modellera systemnära krav och hantera beroenden
* Ta fram krav i form av användningsfall och user stories
* Säkerställa spårbarhet och kvalitet i kravhanteringen
* Samarbeta tätt med verksamhet, utvecklingsteam och andra intressenter

**Kravprofil (ska-krav):**

* Minst 4 års erfarenhet av kravfångst, kravanalys och kravdokumentation
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet av att modellera komplexa, systemnära krav och dess beroenden
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet av att dokumentera krav i form av användningsfall och user stories
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet av arbete i agila utvecklingsmiljöer
* Erfarenhet av modelleringsverktyg, exempelvis Enterprise Architect eller likvärdigt
* Minst 1 års erfarenhet av arbete i Microsoft Azure DevOps Server (ADS) eller Team Foundation Server (TFS)

**Meriterande:**

* Minst 1 års erfarenhet av Enterprise Architect

**Övrigt**  

Vi söker dig som är analytisk, strukturerad och kommunikativ, med förmåga att driva arbetet framåt och skapa tydlighet i komplexa sammanhang.

**Låter detta som din nästa utmaning?**


Vad kul! Skicka in din ansökan så hör vi av oss för ett nästa steg i processen.


Som konsult hos oss på Job Solution Consulting omfattas man av en trygg anställning i nära dialog med sin konsultchef för att alltid säkerställa att du trivs och utvecklas i din roll. Vi erbjuder en rad förmåner med fokus på kompetensutveckling och välmående, Och självklart omfattas man också av kollektivavtal i sin tjänst hos oss. För eventuella frågor eller funderingar är du välkommen att höra av dig till mig.


Du når mig på fredrik.hovstadius@jobsolution.se.

title: Chief Technology Officer
description:
##### **About the role**


Rugvista is looking for a Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead and evolve our technology organisation and define how technology enables growth, efficiency and a best-in-class customer experience.


You will be a key member of the Management Team, reporting to the CEO, and working closely with Product, Commercial, Operations, Finance and Customer Experience to drive business impact. This is a strategic leadership role with a clear mandate to set direction, own key technology decisions, and raise engineering excellence across the organisation. You will ensure our platform remains secure, scalable and reliable as the business evolves and grows.


Our technology organisation is ~14 people, including 3 dedicated Product Owners. This position is based in Malmö (on-site).

##### **Why this role matters now**


Rugvista’s core platform is largely in-house built and tailored to our business, providing control, flexibility and a strong fit. It also comes with trade-offs around complexity, maintenance and long-term scalability.


We are now evaluating moving selected components to external vendors and standard solutions. A central part of your mission is to lead this journey: defining what should remain a competitive differentiator in-house, what we should standardise, and how to execute these changes safely and efficiently without disrupting day-to-day operations.


This requires strong judgment, curiosity, and a willingness to evolve both technology and ways of working in a fast-changing environment.

##### **What you’ll do**

* Set the technology vision and roadmap aligned with Rugvista’s growth ambitions
* Own architecture and platform decisions, balancing speed, scalability, security and cost
* Lead build vs buy decisions, vendor evaluations, and phased migrations or outsourcing of selected components
* Strengthen engineering excellence across delivery practices, quality, DevOps, observability and a culture of ownership
* Partner closely with Product Owners and business stakeholders to prioritise and execute the right initiatives
* Drive responsible AI adoption where it creates measurable value, for example within personalisation, search, operations and customer service
* Ensure security and resilience, including cybersecurity standards, GDPR compliance, incident readiness and business continuity

##### **What you bring**

* Senior tech leadership experience (CTO, VP Engineering, Head of Development or similar) in a fast-paced digital environment
* Strong technical and architectural understanding across modern software development, integrations and cloud infrastructure
* Proven ability to lead teams through change, raising standards while maintaining momentum
* Strong prioritisation and business mindset, with the ability to translate strategy into execution and measurable outcomes
* Clear and confident communicator, able to align technical and non-technical stakeholders
* Fluent in English (Swedish is a plus)
* A valid Swedish work permit


Experience from e-commerce or high-transaction environments, transitioning from in-house systems to vendor platforms, and implementing AI-enabled capabilities is considered a plus


You are curious, pragmatic and outcome-driven. You simplify complexity, challenge assumptions and continuously improve how teams work. You build trust through transparency, calm execution and strong follow-through, especially in a changing environment.

##### **What we offer**

* A key leadership role with real impact on Rugvista’s strategic direction and long-term growth
* The opportunity to lead and shape a critical technology transformation
* A dynamic, fast-paced environment with high ambitions and short decision paths
* A collaborative and value-driven culture, with genuine respect for people and ideas
* Competitive salary and benefits, including wellness allowance and more
* A newly developed office in Malmö, designed for collaboration and performance, including a fully equipped on-site gym

##### **Apply**


Apply with your CV or LinkedIn profile, together with a short note on what attracts you to the role and our technology journey. We are reviewing candidates continuously, so please submit your application as soon as possible.

##### **About Rugvista**


Rugvista is one of Europe’s leading online rug retailers, offering a seamless digital shopping experience across 20 markets. For 20 years, we have built our own brand by combining design, quality and value, from accessible products to high-end handmade rugs.


We are a team of around 100 people, headquartered in Malmö with an additional office in Berlin. Listed on Nasdaq First North Premier Growth Market (RUG), our vision is to become the go-to brand for rugs in Europe.

title: Senior VIL Rig Engineer – Automotive Testing
description:
### **About the Role**


We are looking for an experienced **VIL Rig Engineer** to join a high-performing team within an advanced Software Test Center focused on next-generation automotive systems.


You will play a key role in developing and maintaining **vehicle-in-the-loop (VIL) test environments**, driving automation, and enabling high-quality system-level verification for future vehicle platforms.


This is a hands-on engineering role combining **test development, system analysis, and data-driven validation** in a modern, agile setup.


**This is an urgent requirement — we are looking for candidates who can join immediately or on very short notice.**

### **What You’ll Do**

* Develop and maintain **VIL and rig-based test environments**
* Design and implement **test cases and test sequences** (both high-level and detailed)
* Build and expand **automated test frameworks** in Vector-based environments
* Perform **root cause analysis** of test failures at system and signal level
* Work with **vehicle communication protocols** (CAN, LIN, Ethernet)
* Analyze large datasets and generate **clear test reports, dashboards, and verdicts**
* Collaborate closely with developers, testers, and stakeholders in **cross-functional teams**
* Take ownership of **end-to-end testing activities** within assigned projects

### **Tech Stack / Tools**


You are expected to work with:

* **Vector CANoe / CAPL**
* **Python scripting**
* **Wireshark, DLT, diagnostic tools (DSA, PD)**
* **CAN / LIN / Automotive Ethernet**
* **JIRA / Azure DevOps (Agile / SAFe)**
* **SystemWeaver / CarWeaver (or similar tools)**

### **Your Profile**

* Strong experience in **automotive testing**, ideally with **VIL / HIL / rig environments**
* Solid understanding of **vehicle networks and embedded systems**
* Experience in **test automation and scripting (Python, CAPL)**
* Proven ability to perform **deep technical analysis and debugging**
* Comfortable working in **Agile / SAFe environments**
* Ability to **own and drive technical deliveries independently**
* Experience from **system-level or complete vehicle testing** is highly valued

title: Sourcing Specialist
description:
**About Proxify**


Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Stockholm, Proxify helps 2500+ companies globally to scale their tech teams with top-tier talent in Software, Data, and AI. When internal demand for tech skills arises, Proxify delivers tailored, vetted developers —often within 48 hours— removing traditional hiring bottlenecks and enabling companies to scale faster and smarter.


In 2025, Proxify was highlighted by the Financial Times as the third fastest growing company in Sweden, and took a spot in the Deloitte Fast 50 Sweden in 2024. We have successfully delivered over 700 tech placements to startups, scale-ups, and global enterprises, including names such as Securitas, Electrolux, and Lufthansa.

**About the role**


We are looking for a high-impact Sourcing Specialist who excels in discovering, engaging, and converting top-tier tech talent.


Your **primary mission (#1)** will be **proactive talent sourcing** at scale.


Your **secondary mission (#2)** will be **candidate engagement**, which may include screening, candidate advisory, expectation management, and pipeline stewardship — depending on evolving needs.


You’ll play a strategic role in building our talent supply engine while continuously refining sourcing methodologies to maximize quality and speed.

**What you will do**

**Core Responsibilities**

* Source 40 qualified tech candidates per day (mainly from LinkedIn)
* Build and maintain talent pools for roles such as Full-stack, Backend, Frontend, DevOps, Data, QA, ML/AI, and Security
* Develop advanced sourcing strategies (Boolean, X-ray, market mapping)
* Maintain structured talent pipelines and monitor key sourcing metrics

**Candidate Engagement & Collaboration**

* Conduct structured screening to assess motivation, communication and fit
* Act as a point of contact for candidates throughout the process
* Provide concise candidate assessments and recommendations
* Partner with Recruiters, Client Manager to ensure alignment and quality
* Represent Proxify professionally during all candidate interactions

**Why you’ll love this opportunity at Proxify:**


️ Be part of a humble, supportive and tech-savvy team


Great opportunities for professional development and personal growth


Be part of a global team across 39+ countries


High ownership, high impact: your decisions move the needle

**To succeed in this role, we believe that you:**

**What you must have (Required)**

* 3-5 years of sourcing experience in IT / tech recruitment
* Strong experience in candidate screening & assessment
* Proven ability to source from LinkedIn & open-web channels
* Solid understanding of tech roles (Frontend, Backend, DevOps, Data, etc.)
* Strong command of Boolean / X-ray search
* Excellent communication & stakeholder collaboration
* Fluent English (C1+ level)
* Structured, autonomous, and comfortable working with KPIs

**What is nice to have (Preferred)**

* Experience sourcing for European & global markets
* Familiarity with tools like HireEZ, AmazingHiring, or similar
* Prior experience in a scale-up or high-volume hiring environment
* Experience working with engineers or technical communities
* Basic technical literacy (ability to understand tech concepts and frameworks)

**At Proxify, we believe that great hiring shapes the future.**


With the right people, anything is possible. Are you one of them?


We look forward to meeting you


Please submit your application / CV in English.
